extraction.one_line_summary: YATES Simon attacked from three-rider breakaway with MÜHLBERGER and BILBAO, won tactical sprint after sharp left-hand corner inside final kilometer.
tactical_patterns: YATES Simon deliberately lost time early in Tour to enable breakaway freedom, converted GC irrelevance into stage hunting license.
MÜHLBERGER used barriers to force YATES into predictable attack line in final kilometer, track-style channeling nearly succeeded.
BILBAO Pello least likely sprinter took pulls through finale, fresh legs from weaker sprinter can neutralize faster finishers after big climbs.
GC contenders sat in peloton 68 minutes down, completely uninterested in chasing stage before time trial, enabling massive 40-rider break.

rider_signals.YATES Simon: positive: deliberately sacrificed GC standing early to gain breakaway freedom, executed tactical positioning through final switchback perfectly
rider_signals.MÜHLBERGER Gregor: positive: showed unexpected sprint craft using barriers to channel YATES, nearly stole win despite inferior finishing speed
rider_signals.BILBAO Pello: neutral: strong enough to bridge to leaders but predictably weakest sprinter, used as tactical foil by other two
rider_signals.WELLENS Tim: positive: hunting KoM jersey aggressively by taking every available point on early climbs, 44 points entering Pyrenees
rider_signals.BENOOT Tiesj: neutral: escaped from breakaway group for fourth, showed positioning awareness but missed decisive front split
